frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Arre yaar, job toh theek hai, but once kids come, priorities change na. Father can help, obviously, but chhote bachchon ko maa hi sambhalti hai properly—school, doctor, all that. Career baad mein bhi pick up ho sakti hai, bachpan wapas thodi aata hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it frames childcare as naturally the mother's responsibility while positioning the father as someone who merely 'helps.' It also encodes career sacrifice framing by suggesting a woman's career can wait but childrearing cannot.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Delhi. You are on a matrimonial website helping your younger sister Neha who is 28 create a profile because your parents have been asking you to handle it since Neha is not taking it seriously herself. You are on a phone call with Neha telling her you have created the profile and what you have written and what filters you have set for prospective matches. Neha is not happy that you did this without asking her. You are telling her why you did it anyway and why at 28 she does not have the luxury of not taking this seriously
